creative.concept: Myth vs Fact: 'If it's free, the audio is the product' — debunked with open-source transparency. The mythbuster format pivots the narrative from price to trust, which is the real differentiator vs. free competitors Spokenly and Resonant (neither is open source).

cards.title: Myth vs Fact: 'If it's free, the audio is the product'
cards.use: Primary visual for LinkedIn and X. Export as 1080×1080 PNG. The struck-through myth in muted gray vs. the bold white fact creates an immediate before/after contrast. Jot's GitHub URL at the bottom is the trust signal that makes the claim verifiable.

storyboard.title: Rewrite with voice — speak your instruction, Jot rewrites in place
storyboard.use: Instagram Reel / X video / GIF — Jot's most impressive feature; hasn't been shown since Jun 12. The rewrite-with-voice flow is more visually dramatic than straight dictation and demonstrates a capability most users haven't discovered.

shots.visual: Notes app with a long, wordy paragraph selected (blue highlight).
Hand presses second hotkey (option-shift-Space); Dynamic Island pill appears under Mac notch, pulsing red.
Pill shows audio waveform; user speaks 'Make this shorter and punchier'.
Original paragraph replaced with 2-sentence rewrite; pill vanished.
Wide Notes view; jot-transcribe.com watermark lower-right.

drafts.text:
"If it's free, the audio is the product."

A fair assumption. Except when the app is open source.

Jot: press a hotkey, speak, text at your cursor. Transcription runs on the Apple Neural Engine. No servers. No telemetry. The source is on GitHub -- verify it yourself.

70 days. 30 releases. 229 downloads. $0.

Do you trust free software differently when the code is public?

#macOS #indiedev #buildinpublic
drafts.note: Attach myth-vs-fact.svg (export as 1080x1080 PNG). Do NOT put the link in the post body -- LinkedIn downranks posts with external links. Paste the utm_url as the FIRST COMMENT immediately after posting. The closing question is the engagement trigger. Alternatively, post today's carousel as a LinkedIn document post ('Building Jot: 5 lessons from 70 days of shipping').

drafts.text:
"If it's free, the audio is the product."

Not if it's open source. Jot: hotkey -> speak -> text at cursor. No servers. No telemetry. Audio stays on your Mac. $0.
drafts.note: Attach myth-vs-fact.svg (export as PNG). No link in post -- post the utm_url as the first reply immediately after posting. ~143 characters.

drafts.text:
I open-sourced my free Mac dictation app because I didn't want users to wonder where their audio goes

Building Jot -- a free, open-source Mac dictation app -- I kept running into a trust problem. 'Free apps sell your data' is the default assumption, and it's correct often enough that it's a reasonable prior.

My answer: make everything verifiable. The transcription model runs on the Apple Neural Engine -- no server round-trip, no audio leaving your Mac. The source is on GitHub. There's no ToS for your voice.

Core loop: press a hotkey, speak, text lands wherever your cursor is. Works in any app -- email, Slack, your terminal, your code editor. On-device on Apple Silicon.

Optional AI cleanup and rewrite: Apple Intelligence by default (on-device, macOS 26+), Ollama if you want local-but-not-Apple, or your own API key for cloud. You can also rewrite with voice: select text, press a hotkey, speak the instruction, Jot rewrites in place.

70 days. 30 releases. 11 on-device languages. $0.

Apple Silicon only, macOS 15+. Free and open source on GitHub.

(I built this -- disclosure)

Link in comments.
drafts.note: Post to r/productivity (rotating Jun 25). recommended:false -- last true Jun 23; earliest Jun 27. Answer every comment in the first hour.

drafts.text:
'Free apps sell your data.' Not if they're open source.

Jot runs entirely on your Mac's Neural Engine. No servers. No telemetry. Free, forever.

Link in bio.

#macOS #privacy #dictation #voicetotext #speechtotext #opensource #macapps #indiedev
drafts.note: recommended:false -- last true Jun 23; earliest Jun 26 (tomorrow). Caption links aren't clickable on Instagram -- keep the utm_url (utm_source=instagram) in your bio permanently.

opportunities.title: Dictation not responding on MacBook Pro -- Apple Community
opportunities.why: Active thread about Apple Dictation reliability failures; users expressing frustration; Jot is a stable on-device alternative.
opportunities.suggested_reply: Had this same frustration. I ended up building my own: Jot (jot-transcribe.com) -- free, open-source Mac dictation that runs on the Neural Engine. Works offline, no cloud dependency. (I built it -- disclosure.)

opportunities.title: 9 Best Wispr Flow Alternatives in 2026 -- LumeVoice
opportunities.why: High-SEO roundup; Jot not listed as the only fully-free on-device option. Article appears comment-enabled.
opportunities.suggested_reply: Great list. One worth adding: Jot (jot-transcribe.com) -- the only fully free, open-source on-device option. No subscription, no cloud. 11 languages as of v1.16. I built it. (Disclosure: developer.)

opportunities.title: Wispr Flow Pricing 2026: Plans, Cost, Free Alternative -- Spokenly
opportunities.why: Targets users frustrated by Wispr Flow's $15/month; exact audience for Jot. Flag for outreach Jul 1.

notes: Downloads: 229 total (+5 from yesterday's 224; avg7=11/day). 25 of 30 releases fetched individually via GitHub MCP; 5 minor v1.6.x patches (v1.6.1, v1.6.2, v1.6.4, v1.6.5, v1.6.13) estimated at 13 downloads collectively via backward calculation. iTunes API 403 (13th consecutive day); appstore.json date updated to 2026-06-25, all values preserved. Outreach window closed (last Jun 24; next Jul 1). Reddit recommended:false (last true Jun 23; earliest Jun 27). Instagram recommended:false (last true Jun 23; earliest Jun 26 -- tomorrow). 250-download milestone ~4 days out at current 11/day pace. Resonant continues to dominate Reddit-roundup SEO. Myth-vs-fact format pivots narrative to trust, which is the real differentiator vs. free competitors Spokenly and Resonant (neither is open source). Carousel theme 'Building Jot: 5 lessons from 70 days of shipping' is a strong LinkedIn document-post option.
